objective: Make the FR50 `--verbose`/`-v`/`STAGEHAND_VERBOSE` flag actually emit observable output to stderr — the resolved provider command, the raw agent stdout, and each retry attempt — by (1) threading the resolved verbose setting through `stagehand.GenerateCommit` into the shared `*ui.Output` that drives the executor and the generate orchestrator, and (2) adding the missing `Verbosef` call sites in `internal/provider/executor.go` (resolved command + raw stdout) and `internal/generate/generate.go` (retry attempts). The bug currently leaves stderr empty on a successful commit because `Verbosef` is dead code and `GenerateCommit` hardcodes `verbose=false`. Minimal, focused, pattern-faithful fix; no regressions; no change to the FR51 stdout byte-clean invariant.
context:
## Goal

**Feature Goal**: FR50 verbose output (PRD §15.2, a P1 requirement) is fully functional end-to-end: with `--verbose`/`-v`/`STAGEHAND_VERBOSE=1` set, a successful `stagehand` commit prints to STDERR (a) the fully-resolved provider command, (b) the raw agent stdout, and (c) each generation/retry attempt. With verbose unset, stderr stays empty and stdout is byte-clean (FR51) — identical to today.

**Deliverable**: A minimal, dependency-ordered patch across 4 Go files + 1 doc: (1) `pkg/stagehand/stagehand.go` threads verbose via `Options.Verbose` into one shared `*ui.Output` used by both the executor and `generate.Deps`; (2) `cmd/stagehand/run.go` passes `cfg.Verbose` into `Options`; (3) `internal/provider/executor.go` gains an optional `Output *ui.Output` field and emits the resolved command + raw stdout; (4) `internal/generate/generate.go` emits retry-attempt markers; (5) `docs/CONFIGURATION.md` drops the now-false 'Verbose v1 gap' note.

**Success Definition**: Reproduction from BUG-001 no longer reproduces — `stagehand --provider <name> --verbose 2>/tmp/err.txt` on a successful commit yields a NON-empty `/tmp/err.txt` containing the resolved command, raw stdout, and attempt markers; without `--verbose` it is empty. `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `gofmt -s -l`, and `go test ./...` all pass. `grep -rn '.Verbosef('` over non-test production code returns matches (was zero).

## Why

- FR50 is a P1 PRD requirement; verbose tracing is the ONLY way to debug provider/agent invocation (a tool whose value prop is shelling out to external agent CLIs).
- `Verbosef` is documented as 'the FR50 sink' but is dead code, and `GenerateCommit` constructs its own `*ui.Output` with `verbose=false`, so the flag is a complete no-op today.
- Bug reference: BUG-001 (severity major). Confirmed in `plan/001_f1f80943ac34/bug_hunt_results.json`.

## Root Cause (two independent defects)

1. `pkg/stagehand/stagehand.go` deps literal (~line 209): `Output: ui.NewOutput(os.Stdout, os.Stderr, false, false)` hardcodes `verbose=false`; the resolved `cfg.Verbose` never reaches the generate step.
2. `Verbosef` (defined in `internal/ui/output.go`) has ZERO production call sites — nothing in `internal/provider/executor.go` or `internal/generate/generate.go` ever calls it.

## All Needed Context

### Documentation & References

```yaml
- url: https://go.dev/doc/effective_go#commentary
  why: this codebase uses dense, single-purpose godoc; every new field/method needs a comment in the same style (see existing Output field comments).
- file: internal/ui/output.go
  why: DEFINES Verbosef — the FR50 sink. Verbosef(format, args...): if !o.verbose return nil BEFORE formatting (hot-path short-circuit); else fmt.Fprintf(o.stderr,...). It writes ONLY to stderr (FR51). New call sites MUST route through this method.
  pattern: a method on *Output that self-gates on o.verbose; callers never branch on verbose themselves.
  gotcha: Verbosef does NOT append a newline — the format string owns its trailing \n.
- file: internal/provider/executor.go
  why: owns Run(ctx, m Manifest, model, provider, sys, payload string) (string,error) — the ONLY place the truly-resolved command is known (it resolves DefaultModel/DefaultProvider THEN m.Render). Captures stdout in `var stdout bytes.Buffer` and returns stdout.String().
  pattern: struct Executor{Dir string}; NewExecutor(dir) *Executor; white-box tests construct &Executor{} and inline Manifest literals.
  gotcha: Do NOT change NewExecutor's signature — it is called in ~12 test sites. Do NOT change the Run signature — *Executor satisfies generate.runner STRUCTURALLY and the stub runners in tests must stay unaffected.
- file: internal/generate/generate.go
  why: owns CommitStaged(ctx, deps Deps). The two nested loops (outer dupAttempt 0..cfg.MaxDuplicateRetries, inner parseAttempt 1..2) call `stdout, runErr := deps.Runner.Run(runCtx, deps.Manifest, cfg.Model, cfg.Provider, sys, payload)`. `out` is already in scope (`cfg, out := deps.Config, deps.Output`).
  pattern: out.Progressf / out.Resultf are already used here; add out.Verbosef the same way.
  gotcha: the executor will ALSO emit raw stdout — do NOT re-emit raw stdout in generate.go (avoids duplicate lines).
- file: pkg/stagehand/stagehand.go
  why: the wiring point. Options{} is the public, additive-only API (append-only per the 'Stable as of v1.0' marker). Deps literal at ~line 207 builds the *ui.Output inline with verbose=false.
  pattern: existing additive seams DryRun/SystemExtra were added the same way (P1.M7.T1.S1); a zero Verbose value must be a byte-identical no-op to current behavior.
  gotcha: ui.NewOutput(stdout, stderr, verbose, noColor) — keep noColor=false (color auto-disables on non-TTY/pipe); only verbose is the bug.
- file: cmd/stagehand/run.go
  why: buildOptions(cfg, dryRun) Options is the pure cfg->Options translation; cfg.Verbose is already resolved here (buildFlags folds --verbose/STAGEHAND_VERBOSE via config.Load). runDefault builds its own out with cfg.Verbose but never passes it down.
- file: docs/CONFIGURATION.md
  why: ~line 288 has a `> **Verbose v1 gap.**` note that becomes FALSE after this fix and must be removed/rewritten so docs match behavior.
```

### Architecture constraint (verified)

- The ONLY documented one-way import edge is `config -> provider` (plan_overview.md key decision 1). There is NO rule against `provider -> ui`. `ui` is a leaf package (imports only fmt/io/os), so `provider -> ui` is a clean DAG edge with no cycle. This justifies giving the Executor an optional `*ui.Output`.

### FR51 stream discipline (must NOT break)

- Verbosef -> stderr ONLY. Resultf -> stdout ONLY (FR42 success block + FR49 dry-run message). Progressf -> stderr. Verbose must NEVER touch stdout, so `stagehand --dry-run --no-color | tee /tmp/msg.txt` stays byte-clean.

## Implementation Blueprint

### Threading design (minimal, pattern-faithful)

- Add `Verbose bool` to `stagehand.Options` (APPEND after Timeout; additive-only). Default false => byte-identical no-op to current behavior when unset.
- `buildOptions`: add `Verbose: cfg.Verbose`.
- `GenerateCommit`: construct `out := ui.NewOutput(os.Stdout, os.Stderr, opts.Verbose, false)` ONCE; reuse for both the executor sink and `deps.Output`. Thread it to the executor by setting an exported field.
- `provider.Executor`: add `Output *ui.Output` (nil => silent). Set at the stagehand.go wiring point (`exec := provider.NewExecutor("."); exec.Output = out`). nil-safe => all existing executor/stub/integration tests stay byte-identical.

### Implementation Tasks (ordered by dependencies)

```yaml
Task 1: MODIFY pkg/stagehand/stagehand.go
  - ADD field `Verbose bool` to the Options struct, APPENDED after `Timeout time.Duration`, with a godoc comment in the existing style (e.g. '// Verbose enables FR50 stderr tracing of the resolved command, raw agent stdout, and retry attempts; false is a no-op.'). Keep the 'Stable as of v1.0' additive-only contract.
  - IN GenerateCommit, replace the inline deps Output construction. Build ONE shared sink first:
        out := ui.NewOutput(os.Stdout, os.Stderr, opts.Verbose, false)
        exec := provider.NewExecutor(".")
        exec.Output = out   // FR50 verbose sink (nil-safe; optional field)
    then wire deps.Runner = exec and deps.Output = out (reuse the SAME out for both).
  - UPDATE the wiring-block comment that currently says 'ui.NewOutput uses verbose=false (a library call is not a CLI run)' — it is now misleading; say out honors opts.Verbose and the executor reuses it as the verbose sink.
  - PRESERVE all other Deps fields (Git/Manifest/Config/DryRun/SystemExtra) and the Options layering (Provider/Model/Timeout overrides) byte-for-byte.

Task 2: MODIFY cmd/stagehand/run.go
  - IN buildOptions(cfg, dryRun) Options, add `Verbose: cfg.Verbose` so the resolved verbose flag (from buildFlags -> config.Load) reaches GenerateCommit.
  - REMOVE/REWRITE the 'Known v1 gap (research §1)' doc comment on runDefault that says full FR50 verbose is not observable through the GENERATE step in v1 — it is now fixed.
  - PRESERVE buildFlags, resolveAndCheckProvider, maybeAutoStage, mapErrorToExitCode untouched.

Task 3: MODIFY internal/provider/executor.go
  - IMPORT 'github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/ui' (clean edge, no cycle — ui is a leaf).
  - ADD exported field `Output *ui.Output` to the Executor struct with a godoc comment: optional FR50 verbose sink; nil => silent; set by the wiring layer (pkg/stagehand). Default nil so NewExecutor("") / &Executor{} test call sites are byte-identical.
  - IN Run, AFTER `if model == "" {...}` / `if provider == "" {...}` default-resolution AND after `r, err := m.Render(...)` succeeds, BEFORE building/executing cmd, emit the resolved command:
        if e.Output != nil {
            note := ""
            if r.DeliverViaStdin { note = " (payload via stdin)" }
            e.Output.Verbosef("stagehand: resolved command: %s %s%s\n", m.Command, strings.Join(r.Args, " "), note)
        }
    (strings is already imported.)
  - IN Run, on the SUCCESS path — `case err := <-done: if err == nil { ... }` — BEFORE `return stdout.String(), nil`, emit the raw stdout:
        if e.Output != nil {
            e.Output.Verbosef("stagehand: raw agent stdout:\n%s", stdout.String())
        }
  - DO NOT change Run's signature, the runner interface contract, the timeout/cancel/group-kill logic, or the error paths. Keep the change minimal: 2 guarded Verbosef call sites + 1 field + 1 import.

Task 4: MODIFY internal/generate/generate.go
  - IN CommitStaged's two nested loops, add out.Verbosef markers using loop vars `dupAttempt` (outer) and `parseAttempt` (inner). `out` is already in scope.
  - At the TOP of the inner loop body (before `stdout, runErr := deps.Runner.Run(...)`):
        out.Verbosef("stagehand: generation attempt (duplicate=%d parse=%d)\n", dupAttempt, parseAttempt)
  - After the Parse miss (the `if ok { break }` else-branch where the instruction slot swaps), emit:
        out.Verbosef("stagehand: parse produced no usable message; retrying with correction\n")
  - After a duplicate subject is detected (right before `rejected = append(rejected, subject)`), emit:
        out.Verbosef("stagehand: duplicate subject %q; retrying (duplicate attempt %d)\n", subject, dupAttempt)
  - DO NOT re-emit raw stdout here (the executor already does) — avoids duplicate lines. DO NOT touch the snapshot/CAS/rescue/signal logic.

Task 5: MODIFY docs/CONFIGURATION.md
  - REMOVE/REPLACE the `> **Verbose v1 gap.**` paragraph (~line 288) that claims full FR50 verbose is not observable through the generate step. Replace with a short note that --verbose now prints the resolved command, raw agent stdout, and retries to stderr (FR50), keeping the stdout byte-clean note for --dry-run | tee.

Task 6: ADD unit tests (follow existing white-box patterns)
  - internal/provider/executor_test.go (package provider, uses /bin/echo, /bin/cat as fake agents): ADD a test that sets `e.Output` to a verbose-enabled *ui.Output backed by a bytes.Buffer stderr, runs a /bin/echo command, and asserts stderr contains BOTH the 'resolved command:' line (with /bin/echo) and the 'raw agent stdout:' line (with the echoed text). Also assert that with Output==nil Run behaves exactly as today (stdout returned, no panic).
  - internal/generate/generate_test.go (package generate, stubRunner + stubGit): ADD a test using a verbose-enabled ui.NewOutput(buf-stdout, buf-stderr, verbose=true, noColor=true) in Deps.Output, a stubRunner that returns a duplicate subject then a unique one, and assert buf-stderr contains the 'generation attempt' and 'duplicate subject' markers; and that with verbose=false the buffer is empty.
  - cmd/stagehand/run_test.go: EXTEND TestBuildOptions_DryRunWired (or add a sibling) to assert buildOptions threads `cfg.Verbose` (e.g. cfg.Verbose=true => opts.Verbose==true).
```

### Implementation Patterns & Key Details

```go
// Executor verbose field (internal/provider/executor.go) — optional, nil-safe:
type Executor struct {
    Dir string
    // Output is the optional FR50 verbose sink; nil => silent. Set by the
    // wiring layer (pkg/stagehand.GenerateCommit). Default nil keeps
    // NewExecutor/struct-literal test call sites byte-identical.
    Output *ui.Output
}

// Verbosef call guard — repeat this nil-check at every executor call site:
if e.Output != nil {
    e.Output.Verbosef("stagehand: resolved command: %s %s%s\n",
        m.Command, strings.Join(r.Args, " "), note)
}

// generate.go markers use the already-in-scope `out` (cfg, out := deps.Config, deps.Output):
out.Verbosef("stagehand: generation attempt (duplicate=%d parse=%d)\n", dupAttempt, parseAttempt)
```

### Integration Points

```yaml
CONFIG (no new env vars): --verbose/-v/STAGEHAND_VERBOSE already resolve into cfg.Verbose via buildFlags -> config.Load. No new flag/env needed.
PUBLIC API (additive-only): stagehand.Options gains Verbose bool (append after Timeout). Zero value is a no-op — existing Options{} callers byte-identical.
DOCS: docs/CONFIGURATION.md 'Verbose v1 gap' note removed/rewritten.
```

## Validation Loop

### Level 1 — Syntax/Type/Vet/Fmt (run after each file; one command per gate)
- `go build ./...`
- `go vet ./...`
- `test -z "$(gofmt -s -l internal/ cmd/ pkg/)"`   # prints nothing & exits 0 when formatted

### Level 2 — Unit tests (component validation)
- `go test ./internal/generate/ ./internal/provider/ ./internal/ui/ ./pkg/stagehand/ ./cmd/stagehand/`   # affected packages
- `go test ./...`   # full suite (must stay green; no regressions)

### Level 3 — End-to-end reproduction (proves the bug is fixed)
Build the binary, set up a scratch git repo with a staged change + a working stub provider (see internal/generate/testdata/stubagent), then:
- WITH verbose: `./bin/stagehand --provider <stub> --verbose 2>/tmp/err.txt` — assert /tmp/err.txt is NON-empty and contains 'resolved command', 'raw agent stdout', and 'generation attempt'.
- WITHOUT verbose: `./bin/stagehand --provider <stub> 2>/tmp/err2.txt` — assert /tmp/err2.txt is empty and stdout carries the FR42 success block (byte-clean).
- `-v` and `STAGEHAND_VERBOSE=1` must behave identically to `--verbose`.

## Final Validation Checklist

### Technical
- [ ] `go build ./...` succeeds.
- [ ] `go vet ./...` is clean.
- [ ] `gofmt -s -l internal/ cmd/ pkg/` lists nothing.
- [ ] `go test ./...` is green (no regressions; new verbose tests pass).

### Feature (FR50)
- [ ] `grep -rn '.Verbosef(' internal/provider internal/generate pkg/stagehand cmd/stagehand | grep -v _test.go` returns matches (was zero).
- [ ] `stagehand --verbose` writes resolved command + raw stdout + attempts to STDERR on success.
- [ ] Without --verbose, stderr is empty and stdout is byte-clean (FR51) — unchanged behavior.
- [ ] cfg.Verbose flows: --verbose -> buildFlags -> config.Load -> cfg.Verbose -> buildOptions -> Options.Verbose -> GenerateCommit -> ui.NewOutput(opts.Verbose,...) -> executor.Output + deps.Output.

### Code quality
- [ ] Options.Verbose appended (additive-only; zero value is a no-op).
- [ ] NewExecutor signature UNCHANGED; Run signature UNCHANGED; runner interface UNCHANGED.
- [ ] Executor.Output is nil-safe (existing executor/stub/integration tests byte-identical).
- [ ] No duplicate raw-stdout emission (executor emits it once).
- [ ] Comments updated (stagehand.go wiring note, run.go 'Known v1 gap', docs 'Verbose v1 gap').

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- Do NOT change NewExecutor's signature (breaks ~12 test call sites).
- Do NOT change Run's signature or the generate.runner interface (breaks structural typing + stub runners).
- Do NOT branch on verbose in callers — always go through out.Verbosef (it self-gates).
- Do NOT write verbose to stdout (violates FR51 byte-clean invariant).
- Do NOT hardcode verbose=true; it must default to false and be a no-op when unset.
- Do NOT re-emit raw stdout in both executor AND generate.go (pick executor; generate.go emits retry markers only).
- Do NOT remove/rename existing Options fields (additive-only stability contract).
- Do NOT catch broad exceptions or alter error/rescue/CAS paths — this fix is additive tracing only.
